title: Adobe adds AI assistant to Photoshop, upgrades Firefly with new generative tools
snippet: Adobe is launching a beta AI assistant for Photoshop on web and mobile, allowing paid users unlimited generations until April 9. The assistant simplifies editing tasks through voice or text commands, while Firefly Image Editor receives updates for improved image manipulation.

title: Google turns Chrome into an AI browser with Gemini support in India — top features, how to use and more
snippet: Google is enhancing Chrome with Gemini AI integration. The feature is now available to users in India, Canada, and New Zealand. The AI can be used to summarize content, draft emails, and edit images.&nbsp;

title: Xiaomi 17, Xiaomi 17 Ultra with LEICA camera setup launched in India: Price, specs, launch offers and more
snippet: Xiaomi launched the 17 series in India, featuring LTPO 120Hz displays, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processors, and LEICA camera setup. The Xiaomi 17 starts at  ₹89,999, while the Ultra model begins at  ₹1,39,999

title: Instagram down? Thousands report issues accessing social media platform, frustrated netizens say ‘whole thing is broken’
snippet: Instagram is experiencing access issues, with over 10,000 user reports on Downdetector. The peak of complaints occurred around 8:59 AM India time.

title: Incentives, value addition boost made-in-India laptops
snippet: Driven by government incentives, Made in India laptops now account for nearly 30% of domestic sales, up from less than 5% in 2024. Local manufacturers like Dixon and Syrma SGS are aggressively scaling capacity to offset a slowdown in the smartphone sector.

title: Michael Burry claims Nvidia used ‘mafia-like’ tactics to block AMD in Oracle data center deal, calls for antitrust probe
snippet: Despite cancelling the planned 600-MW expansion at the Abilene site, Oracle and OpenAI’s broader plan to build 4.5 gigawatts of AI data centre capacity remains on track.

title: iPhone Fold leaks: Expected price, release date, specifications and more
snippet: Apple's upcoming foldable iPhone, likely called iPhone Ultra, is likely to launch later this year. The phone could feature a dual rear camera setup and four cameras in total.&nbsp;

title: Russia-backed hackers target Signal and WhatsApp accounts: How the attack works, how to stay safe and more
snippet: Dutch intelligence agencies warned of a global cyber campaign by Russian-backed hackers targeting Signal and WhatsApp accounts of officials and journalists. Hackers are using phishing techniques to gain access to the accounts, urging victims to provide SMS codes or PINs.

title: Xiaomi Pad 8 with Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 processor, 9,200mAh battery launched in India: Price, specs and more
snippet: Xiaomi has launched the Pad 8 tablet in India, featuring a Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 processor, a 9200mAh battery, and a 144Hz LCD display. The tablet starts at a price of  ₹33,999 in India.

title: iPhone 18 Pro, iPhone 18 Pro Max launching this year: Expected price, specs and more
snippet: Apple's upcoming iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max are expected to retain similar display sizes while featuring under-display Face ID and a new unified design. Here's everything to expect from the new devices

Code-gen startup Cursor in talks for $50 billion valuation: Bloomberg
snippet: Code-generation startup Cursor is discussing a new funding round with investors that could value the company at about $50 billion, Bloomberg News reported. That would be a sharp rise from its $29.3 billion valuation after raising $2.3 billion in the previous round in November.

snippet: The recent conflict ​between the ​US government and Anthropic ⁠temporarily affected discussions over the joint venture, but talks are ongoing, according to The Information report. title: Oracle's stake in TikTok US joint venture is worth roughly $2 billion, filing shows
snippet: Oracle’s stake in TikTok’s U.S. operations is valued at about $2 billion after ByteDance created a majority-owned American joint venture to avoid a ban. US and global investors hold 80.1% while Oracle, Silver Lake and MGX each own 15%. Meanwhile, Oracle said the AI data centre boom could lift revenu

title: SoftBank's PayPay prices IPO below range at $16 a share
snippet: PayPay on Wednesday priced ​its US initial public offering ​at $16 per share, below its targeted price range, as the US-Israeli war with ​Iran weighed on market sentiment. The IPO raised about $880 million based on 55 million American depositary receipts sold, valuing the Japanese digital wallet pro

title: Atlassian to cut roughly 10% jobs in pivot to AI affecting 16% of Indian employees
snippet: The company said 40% of affected employees are in North America, followed by 30% in Australia and 16% in India. It expects to incur about $225 million to $236 million in charges related ​to the layoffs and office space reductions.

title: Samsung Display CEO warns of higher costs due to Middle East conflict, media say
snippet: ​Samsung ​Display's chief executive said on ‌Thursday ⁠the ⁠war ​in the Middle ​East threatens to ​drive ⁠up costs ‌of ​energy ​and ⁠raw materials needed ​for the flat ​screens used in ‌mobile phones ​and ​other ⁠electronic devices, media reported.

title: Pentagon opens door to exempt Anthropic use beyond 6-month ramp-down, memo says
snippet: The memo comes after a ‌heated weeks-long dispute over technology guardrails on the use of Anthropic's AI tools by the military that culminated in Defense Secretary Pete ​Hegseth labelling ​the firm a supply ⁠chain risk and banning its use by the Pentagon and its contractors.

title: Netflix to pay as much as $600 million for AI filmmaking firm InterPositive: Bloomberg
snippet: Netflix may pay up to $600 million for Ben Affleck’s AI filmmaking company InterPositive, though the cash price is lower with extra earnings tied to performance targets. The deal suggests Hollywood is warming to AI-created content. Founded in 2022, InterPositive uses AI to maintain cinematic logic a

title: Musk unveils joint Tesla-xAI project 'Macrohard', eyes software disruption
snippet: Musk's xAI was previously building Macrohard as ​an AI ​project that would allow developers to simulate software ⁠creations by companies like Microsoft.
